codeReview.summary: The feature adds a substantial settings/device-management surface and wires access-mode gating into the mobile UI, but it does not actually hook notification preferences into any notification-delivery path and its "Recent security events" list shows the oldest entries first rather than the most recent activity.

issues.description: The new notification preferences are only persisted locally; they are never connected to a notification delivery pipeline. The only runtime consumers are `apps/mobile/lib/features/threads/presentation/thread_list_page.dart:77`, which hides the approvals badge, and `apps/mobile/lib/features/threads/presentation/thread_detail_page.dart:101`, which shows an informational banner. That means enabling/disabling the toggles does not actually deliver or suppress approval/live-activity notifications as required by VAL-SETTINGS-004.

The "Recent security events" section takes the first 8 events from the bridge response without reordering them. Bridge security events are appended in insertion order (`crates/bridge-core/src/logging.rs:30-34`) and exposed as-is (`crates/bridge-core/src/lib.rs:766-776`), so once more than eight events exist this UI drops the newest sensitive actions and shows the oldest ones instead of the most recent history the feature promises.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `mobile-worker` skill still requires a manual verification pass and recording visible behavior, but this feature handoff only captured automated `commandsRun` and still marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` as true. Either the skill should explicitly permit automation-only completion for this kind of UI work, or the handoff guidance should require workers to record the missing manual check as a deviation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/.factory/skills/mobile-worker/SKILL.md:23-24 says to manually verify the flow and record exact visible behavior, including `interactiveChecks`; the handoff at /Users/lubomirmolin/.factory/missions/5e6e6a03-5030-4a14-862e-f91b0b989525/handoffs/2026-03-18T10-54-02-893Z__mobile-settings-access-modes-and-unpair__89b3ee66-b155-4c81-976a-33acd8785c5c.json:14-59 lists only `commandsRun`, and :62 still marks `followedProcedure` true.

summary: Reviewed the worker transcript skeleton, handoff, skill, mission docs, and commit `de42be3`. Result: fail, because notification-preference toggles do not yet control notification delivery; also noted that the security-events list surfaces oldest-first entries and a mobile-worker skill/handoff mismatch around manual verification evidence.
